records.ety: [OE. bisegen; pref. be- + segen to siege. See Siege.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Besieged (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Besieging.]

defs: To beset or surround with armed forces, for the purpose of compelling to surrender; to lay siege to; to beleaguer; to beset.
quotes: Till Paris was besieged, famished, and lost.
